THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED AS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², WHERE R IS THE RADII OF THE CIRCLE. THE RADII OF THE CIRCLES ARE IN A 10% INCREASED SERIES, STARTING WITH R. THE AREAS OF THE CIRCLES ARE πR², π(1.1R)², π(1.21R)², ..., π(1.771561R)². THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF THESE AREAS.

THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS DETERMINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF A CIRCLE IS 2πR. THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS IS 0.5 CM. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY THE GAP. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2π(1.1R) = 2.2πR. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 2.2πR / 0.5 = 4.4πR. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 44πR. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 44πR.
